title.value: Karmein Chan has glandular fever

summary.value: Karmein Chan has glandular fever, also known as infectious mononucleosis, and receives treatment at the Royal Children’s Hospital of Melbourne.

title.value: Melbourne Cup takes place at Flemington Racecourse

summary.value: The Melbourne Cup, a 3.2 km handicap horse race, takes place at Flemington Racecourse.

title.value: Karmein camps at Jerusalem Creek with PLC classmates

summary.value: Karmein Chan is camping at Jerusalem Creek with her classmates from Presbyterian Ladies’ College.

title.value: Dissolution of the Soviet Union

summary.value: Timeline contextual entry: Dissolution of the Soviet Union.

summary.value: Easter Monday public holiday in Victoria/Australia context.

title.value: Last school day of term 1

summary.value: Last school day of term 1

title.value: Tennis lesson for Karmein at Boroondara Tennis Centre

summary.value: Karmein attends a tennis lesson at the Boroondara Tennis Centre, formerly Camberwell Tennis Centre.

title.value: Phyllis and Karmein have breakfast at Bulleen Plaza

summary.value: Phyllis and Karmein have breakfast at the Bulleen Plaza shopping centre; the timeline notes cappuccino and orange juice.

title.value: Karmein does homework at Bulleen Library

summary.value: Karmein does her homework at the Bulleen Library, approximately between 10:30 AM and 12:00 PM.

title.value: Family friends collect the Chan sisters and drive them to Ming’s

summary.value: A married couple, friends of the Chans, collected Karmein and her sisters, who had been at a Chinese (Mandarin) lesson, and drove them to Ming’s.

review_flags: This source sentence combines Karmein’s Bulleen Library homework with the sisters’ collection/transfer; split further if primary-source detail improves.

title.value: Lunch at Eltham restaurant

summary.value: Approximate lunch at the Eltham restaurant operated by the Chan family.

title.value: Karmein, Karly and Karen play outside the restaurant and have dinner

summary.value: Karmein, Karly and Karen play outside the Eltham restaurant and have dinner.

title.value: Eltham restaurant manager drives the three sisters home

summary.value: The Eltham restaurant manager drives Karmein, Karly and Karen home.

review_flags: The website timeline explicitly notes: 'Conflicts with KCSRC00005'.

title.value: The girls are at home with John Chan

summary.value: Karmein, Karly and Karen are at home with their father John Chan.

title.value: John Chan goes to work at Ming’s restaurant in Eltham

summary.value: John Chan leaves the family home and goes to work at Ming’s restaurant in Eltham.

title.value: Karmein bathes her sisters, reads stories, and the girls watch a Marilyn Monroe documentary

summary.value: Karmein bathes her younger sisters and reads them bedtime stories. They start watching 'Marilyn: Something’s Got To Give' on Channel 10.

title.value: Karmein and Karly encounter the intruder in the family hallway

summary.value: Karmein and Karly encounter a man in a green tracksuit and balaclava wielding a long knife in the family hallway; he ties the younger sisters and locks them in a wardrobe.

title.value: Karmein Chan is abducted

summary.value: Karmein is abducted, barefoot and wearing a white short-sleeved knee-length nightdress with a blue flower pattern.

title.value: Karmein’s trail is lost after the abduction

summary.value: From the point of the abduction onward, Karmein’s trail is lost.

review_flags: Status statement rather than a discrete witnessed event.

title.value: Karly and Karen break free and call John Chan

summary.value: Karly and Karen break free and call their father at the restaurant.

title.value: John Chan leaves his restaurant in Eltham

summary.value: John Chan leaves his restaurant in Eltham after receiving the call.

title.value: John Chan arrives at 113 Serpells Road

summary.value: John Chan arrives at the Chan family home at 113 Serpells Road, Templestowe.

title.value: Police are called

summary.value: Police are called after John Chan arrives home and discovers Karmein is missing.

title.value: Sergeant Rodney David Phillips arrives at the scene

summary.value: Sergeant Rodney David Phillips from Victoria Police is at the scene and meets John Chan, Karly and Karen next to the garage.

title.value: Chan family taken to Doncaster police station

summary.value: The Chan family is taken to Doncaster police station to make their first statements.

title.value: Karmein has been missing as long as Sharon Wills had been missing

summary.value: Karmein Chan has now been missing for as long as Sharon Wills was in December 1988.

title.value: Karmein has been missing as long as Nicola Lynas had been missing

summary.value: Karmein Chan has now been missing for as long as Nicola Lynas was in July 1990.

title.value: John and Phyllis Chan give their first press conference

summary.value: John and Phyllis Chan give their first press conference after Karmein’s abduction.

title.value: Karly and Karen Chan write letters to Karmein

summary.value: Karly and Karen Chan each write a letter to their missing sister.

title.value: Victorian government offers $100,000 reward

summary.value: The Victorian government offers a $100,000 reward, described by the timeline as unusually fast and substantial.

title.value: Police conduct large-scale door knock near Chan residence

summary.value: Police conduct a large-scale door knock of around 2,500 homes near the Chans’ Templestowe residence.

title.value: First school day of term 2

summary.value: First school day of term 2.

title.value: Presbyterian Ladies’ College students return to school

summary.value: Presbyterian Ladies’ College students return to school.

title.value: Phyllis Chan writes a letter to “Mr Kind”

summary.value: Phyllis Chan writes a letter to 'Mr Kind'.

title.value: Patronage at the family’s Chinese restaurants has dropped dramatically

summary.value: By this date, patronage at the family’s two Chinese restaurants has dropped off dramatically.

title.value: Karmein Chan’s classmates write to her family

summary.value: Karmein Chan’s classmates write a letter to her family to express their sympathy.

title.value: Phyllis Chan addresses a letter to the man who is with Karmein

summary.value: Phyllis Chan addresses a letter to 'the man who is with Karmein Chan now'.

title.value: Karly Chan has started to sleepwalk while calling out for Karmein

summary.value: Karly Chan has started to sleepwalk while calling out for her missing sister.

title.value: Phyllis Chan publishes a coded letter in the Sunday Herald Sun

summary.value: Phyllis Chan publishes a coded letter in the Sunday Herald Sun.

title.value: Phyllis Chan publishes another letter in the Herald Sun on Karmein’s 14th birthday

summary.value: Phyllis Chan publishes another letter in the Herald Sun. Karmein would have been 14 years old.

title.value: Karmein Chan’s body is found in Thomastown

summary.value: Karmein’s body is found in Thomastown by a man walking his dog at around 5 PM.

title.value: Service held at Bulleen Baptist Church

summary.value: A service is held at Bulleen Baptist Church.

title.value: Karmein Chan’s remains are cremated in Fawkner

summary.value: Later in the day, Karmein’s remains are cremated in Fawkner.

title.value: PLC memorial service at Scots Church

summary.value: About 700 students and teachers from Presbyterian Ladies’ College attend a memorial service at Scots Church in Melbourne.

title.value: The offender was never brought to justice

summary.value: The offender was never brought to justice.

review_flags: Undated status statement from the timeline, not a discrete dated event.
